title: Sudden tearing chest pain to the back

chart.oneLiner: 58M with poorly controlled hypertension presents with sudden, severe, tearing chest pain radiating to the back that began 1 hour ago.

opening: It came on all at once — the worst pain of my life, like something ripped in my chest and went right through to my back.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 58-year-old man, in severe distress, restless, sweating. Frightened.

HPI:
- Sudden, MAXIMAL-at-onset, severe chest pain ~1 hour ago, described as TEARING/RIPPING, radiating straight through to the back between the shoulder blades.
- Pain was at its worst immediately (not crescendo). Migrated downward toward the abdomen over time (only if asked).
- NO exertional trigger, NO relief with rest, NO pleuritic quality, NO cough/fever.
- Known hypertension, frequently misses his medications and hasn't checked his BP in months (only mentions poor control if asked about adherence).

OLDCARTS: abrupt onset 1h ago, maximal immediately; retrosternal radiating to back; constant; tearing/ripping; not positional or pleuritic; severity 10/10.

PMH: hypertension (poorly controlled), hyperlipidemia. MEDICATIONS: lisinopril and amlodipine — frequently nonadherent. ALLERGIES: none. FAMILY: father had an 'aneurysm' (only if asked). SOCIAL: current smoker ~40 pack-years, occasional alcohol.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY the system/maneuver requested; give normals when asked):
- General: diaphoretic, in severe pain, restless.
- Vitals: BP 188/104 in the RIGHT arm, 150/82 in the LEFT arm (pulse/pressure differential); HR 96.
- Cardiac: regular; soft early diastolic murmur at the left sternal border if specifically auscultated (aortic regurgitation).
- Lungs: clear bilaterally.
- Pulses: right radial fuller than left; both lower extremity pulses present but diminished if checked.
- Chest wall: NO reproducible tenderness. Abdomen: soft, mild discomfort. Neuro: intact.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis.
- Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the medication nonadherence, the smoking, the family history, or that the pain migrated unless specifically asked.
- The BP differential and the murmur are only revealed if the student measures BP in both arms or auscultates the heart.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.ctangio: CT angiogram of the chest: intimal flap in the ascending aorta extending into the arch — Stanford type A aortic dissection.

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential diagnosis (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Aortic dissection (leading & CANT-MISS, 10; accepts dissecting aneurysm). Acute coronary syndrome / STEMI (CANT-MISS,5). Pulmonary embolism (CANT-MISS,3). Pericarditis (core,2). Esophageal rupture (bonus,2). Names dissection and the features that point to it — tearing pain, BP differential, abrupt maximal onset (core,4).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Aortic dissection. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Acute coronary syndrome / STEMI; Pulmonary embolism. Also reasonable: Pericarditis.

coreActions.item: Names dissection and the features that point to it — tearing pain, BP differential, abrupt maximal onset

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What workup do you want? (And what must you check at the bedside?)
steps.rubric: Bilateral arm BP / pulse exam (CANT-MISS,3). EKG to exclude STEMI (core,2). CXR (core,2). CT angiogram of chest/aorta (CANT-MISS, 8; accepts CTA or TEE). Troponin (core,1). Type and crossmatch blood (bonus,1). PENALTY: anticoagulating before excluding dissection (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; CBC; BMP; Troponin; D-dimer; CT angiography.

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage with a lateral strain pattern; NO acute STEMI. CREDIT: applies LVH voltage criteria (4); EXPLICITLY recognizes the strain repolarization changes are NOT an acute STEMI - essential before any anticoagulation/thrombolysis (7); systematic read (2). PENALTY: misreading the strain pattern as a STEMI and giving thrombolytics/anticoagulation (-3).
keyFindings: recognizes a strain pattern, not acute STEMI
criticalFindings: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
commonMistakes: reading the strain repolarization changes as acute ischemia/STEMI
steps.idealAnswer: Left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage with a lateral strain pattern; not an acute STEMI.

criticalActions: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
coreActions: recognizes a strain pattern, not acute STEMI

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = WIDENED MEDIASTINUM with an enlarged aortic knob. CREDIT: identifies the widened mediastinum (6); links it to suspected aortic dissection (4). PENALTY: calling the film normal / missing the widened mediastinum (-3).
keyFindings: links it to suspected aortic dissection
commonMistakes: calling the film normal / missing the widened mediastinum

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Widened mediastinum; enlarged aortic knob.

coreActions.item: links it to suspected aortic dissection

penalties.item: calling the film normal / missing the widened mediastinum

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Given the widened mediastinum and CT findings, what is your diagnosis and why does the location matter?
steps.rubric: Stanford type A aortic dissection (8; accepts ascending aortic dissection). Recognizes type A involves the ascending aorta and is a surgical emergency (4). Notes complications — aortic regurgitation, tamponade, malperfusion (3).

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH Acute Aortic Syndromes: rate control FIRST with IV beta blocker (esmolol/labetalol), HR <60 (CANT-MISS,6); THEN BP control SBP 100-120, nitroprusside after beta blockade (5); STAT cardiothoracic surgery consult (CANT-MISS,5); type A → emergent surgery, type B → medical (3); pain control (2); large-bore IV access + type & screen (2). PENALTY: vasodilator (nitroprusside) before beta blocker → reflex tachycardia/shear (-3); anticoagulation/thrombolytics (-4).
steps.idealAnswer: Emergency. Control heart rate first (IV beta blocker, e.g. esmolol, target HR <60) THEN blood pressure (target SBP 100–120, add nitroprusside) to reduce aortic wall stress. Pain control. STAT cardiac surgery consult – Stanford type A (ascending) is a surgical emergency; type B (descending) is usually managed medically. Type & screen, two large-bore IVs.

criticalActions.item: IV beta blocker for rate control (HR<60)

coreActions.item: Type A surgical / type B medical
Large-bore IV access and type & screen

requiredActions: IV beta blocker for rate control (HR<60)
optionalActions: Type A surgical / type B medical
Large-bore IV access and type & screen

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Voltage criteria for LVH (S in V1 + R in V5/V6 >35 mm) with lateral strain (ST depression / T inversion I, aVL, V5-6). No acute STEMI.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Voltage criteria for LVH (S in V1 + R in V5/V6 >35 mm) with lateral strain (ST depression / T inversion I, aVL, V5-6). No acute STEMI. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray
cxr.verify: Pick a CC chest film matching: Widened mediastinum; enlarged aortic knob..
searchTerms: chest radiograph Widened mediastinum; enlarged aortic

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 58M with poorly controlled hypertension presents with sudden, severe, tearing chest pain radiating to the back that began 1 hour ago. The diagnosis is Aortic dissection, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Cardiac: regular; soft early diastolic murmur at the left sternal border if specifically auscultated (aortic regurgitation).
General: diaphoretic, in severe pain, restless.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Aortic dissection while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Aortic dissection and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
managementPearls: IV beta blocker for rate control (HR<60)
criticalInterpretationItems: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
criticalManagementItems: IV beta blocker for rate control (HR<60)
keyImageInterpretationFindings: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
criticalImageInterpretationFindings: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
